name: Raw dough
description: Raw yeast dough is toxic to cats in two distinct ways. The live yeast ferments sugars in the warm stomach environment, producing carbon dioxide gas and ethanol. The CO2 causes painful abdominal bloating and can distend the stomach to dangerous levels, while the absorbed ethanol causes rapid alcohol poisoning.
safetyNotes: Toxic mechanism 1: yeast fermentation produces CO2 — abdominal bloating, distension, and risk of gastric dilation
Toxic mechanism 2: ethanol production from fermentation — rapidly absorbed; cats lack alcohol dehydrogenase, causing CNS and respiratory depression
Symptoms: distended abdomen, vomiting, lethargy, disorientation, weakness; ethanol intoxication causes unsteady gait, low body temperature, slow breathing
Baked bread is safe — the heat kills yeast and evaporates alcohol; only raw dough poses this risk
Contact your vet immediately if your cat ingests raw yeast dough

symptoms.name: Gastric Distension (Bloating) and Abdominal Pain
symptoms.onset: Early (often within the first few hours).
symptoms.notes: When ingested, the warm, moist environment of the cat's stomach acts as an incubator for the yeast in the raw dough12. The yeast ferments dietary carbohydrates, producing large amounts of carbon dioxide gas that causes the dough mass to rapidly expand23. Because of its sticky, glutinous texture, it

symptoms.name: Neurological Impairment (Ataxia and Disorientation)
symptoms.notes: As a byproduct of fermentation, the yeast also produces ethanol (alcohol) directly inside the stomach12. This ethanol is rapidly absorbed into the bloodstream and easily crosses the blood-brain barrier67. Because cats lack robust enzymes to safely metabolize alcohol, this causes profound central ner

symptoms.name: Respiratory Distress and Depression
symptoms.onset: Progressive (within 1 hour to a few hours).
symptoms.notes: Respiratory issues in raw dough toxicosis arise from two concurrent dangers. First, the physically distended stomach presses heavily against the diaphragm, which impairs the cat's normal breathing ability25. Second, as ethanol intoxication worsens, it directly suppresses the central nervous system's

symptoms.name: Hypothermia
symptoms.notes: The ethanol absorbed from the raw dough induces peripheral vasodilation (widening of the blood vessels near the skin's surface)6. This causes the cat's body to rapidly lose heat, dropping its core temperature to dangerous levels

symptoms.name: Metabolic Acidosis and Hypoglycemia
symptoms.onset: Progressive (usually within an hour and worsening over time).
symptoms.notes: The systemic absorption and partial metabolism of the ethanol produce acetic and lactic acids, which dangerously lower the pH of the cat's blood (metabolic acidosis)67. The intoxication can also trigger severe hypoglycemia8. Ultimately, death from raw dough toxicosis is usually due to these systemic

symptoms.name: Severe CNS Depression (Seizures and Coma)
symptoms.onset: Progressive (as fermentation continues and ethanol levels peak).
symptoms.notes: Because the raw dough continues to actively ferment inside the stomach, it acts as an ongoing, continuous source of alcohol2. Without rapid veterinary intervention, the initial weakness and disorientation will progress to recumbency, seizures, a comatose state, and death
